target_audience: A framework operator judging whether benchmark quality improved across actual self-hosting runtime loops rather than only synthetic fixtures.

loop_trace.main_blind_spot: The run proves a single broken-chain family and only human-readable audit evidence, so the operator still lacks stronger machine-readable inspection support.
The run adds machine-readable audit evidence and cost thresholds, but still covers only one broken-chain family.

loop_trace.key_correction: The run keeps the positive path green while adding a second broken-chain family, making the negative-path benchmark coverage materially stronger.

expected_pass_condition: The verdict must explain that loop 2 improved audit structure, while loop 3 introduced the substantive negative-coverage correction.
expected_fail_condition: Any verdict that treats loop 2 and loop 3 as equivalent, or cannot distinguish structural polish from broader failure-family coverage, should fail.
